pages.seo_title: Is Google Down? Google Status Check (Live)
pages.meta_title: Google Status Check – Is Google Down Right Now?
pages.meta_description: Check if Google is down or working. Live real-time Google status updates from LegalStatusCheck. Updated every 60 seconds.
pages.intro: Wondering if Google is down right now? LegalStatusCheck monitors Google's search engine and productivity suite in real time so you can instantly see whether it's operational or facing disruption.
pages.status_section:
🟢 Google Online — All systems operational
🟡 Google Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 Google Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: Server overload caused by an unexpected traffic spike
Scheduled or emergency maintenance window
DNS resolution failure preventing domain lookup
Regional network outage affecting specific geographies
Database connectivity issue on the backend

faq.q: Is this service down right now?
faq.a: Check the live status indicator at the top of this page. Green means fully operational, yellow means degraded performance, and red means a confirmed outage is in progress.

faq.q: Why is it not loading for me but working for others?
faq.a: This is usually caused by a regional outage, ISP routing issue, or a local DNS cache problem. Try flushing your DNS cache or connecting via a VPN to test from a different location.

faq.q: How often is the status data updated?
faq.a: LegalStatusCheck checks service availability every 60 seconds. The timestamp on this page shows exactly when the last check was performed.

faq.q: What causes most outages?
faq.a: The most common causes are server overload, failed deployments, DNS failures, and upstream infrastructure issues from cloud providers.

faq.q: What should I do while the service is down?
faq.a: Check the service's official status page or social media for updates. Use a cached version of your data if available, and set up an alert on this page to be notified when it recovers.

pages.seo_title: Is YouTube Down? YouTube Status Check (Live)
pages.meta_title: YouTube Status Check – Is YouTube Down Right Now?
pages.meta_description: Check if YouTube is down or working. Live real-time YouTube status updates from LegalStatusCheck. Updated every 60 seconds.
pages.intro: Use this page to check whether YouTube is currently offline or experiencing service issues. Our monitoring system tracks YouTube's video streaming platform around the clock.
pages.status_section:
🟢 YouTube Online — All systems operational
🟡 YouTube Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 YouTube Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: DDoS attack or sudden surge in request volume
Third-party API dependency failure
SSL certificate expiry or misconfiguration
Cloud infrastructure provider incident
Deployment or code push gone wrong

faq.q: How do I know if the outage is widespread?
faq.a: If our monitoring shows a red status, the issue is confirmed from multiple monitoring locations worldwide — it's not just you. Yellow status means partial or regional issues.

faq.q: Can I get notified when it comes back online?
faq.a: Yes — LegalStatusCheck offers status alerts. Bookmark this page and check back, or use the notification feature to receive an alert when service is restored.

faq.q: Is the status check real-time?
faq.a: Our checks run every minute from multiple global locations. The status you see reflects the most recent check, never more than 60 seconds old.

faq.q: Why does the service keep going down?
faq.a: Repeated outages usually indicate infrastructure scaling issues, ongoing incidents with a cloud provider, or architectural problems the team is actively working to resolve.

faq.q: Who do I contact if the service is down?
faq.a: Contact the service's official support team directly. While we track uptime, we have no ability to expedite recovery — only the service operator can resolve backend issues.

pages.seo_title: Is Instagram Down? Instagram Status Check (Live)
pages.meta_title: Instagram Status Check – Is Instagram Down Right Now?
pages.meta_description: Check if Instagram is down or working. Live real-time Instagram status updates from LegalStatusCheck. Updated every 60 seconds.
pages.intro: If Instagram isn't loading for you, you're in the right place. LegalStatusCheck provides live uptime data for Instagram, a photo and video sharing network, updated continuously.
pages.status_section:
🟢 Instagram Online — All systems operational
🟡 Instagram Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 Instagram Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: CDN edge node failure or cache poisoning
Internal load balancer misconfiguration
Storage or disk I/O bottleneck on the server
Authentication service outage blocking logins
Firewall rule change blocking legitimate traffic

faq.q: Does this page show historical uptime data?
faq.a: Yes — the uptime history grid shows the last 90 days of availability. Green cells indicate operational days, yellow indicates degraded performance, and red marks confirmed outages.

faq.q: Is it down just in my country?
faq.a: Our monitoring runs from multiple regions. If the status shows green but the service is unavailable to you, it may be a geo-restricted outage or a local ISP issue specific to your region.

faq.q: How accurate is the uptime percentage?
faq.a: The uptime percentage reflects our own monitoring data based on continuous checks. It may differ slightly from the service provider's own reported uptime metrics.

faq.q: What does 'degraded performance' mean?
faq.a: Degraded performance means the service is technically reachable but responding slowly, timing out on certain requests, or experiencing errors on some — but not all — functionality.

faq.q: Can I report an issue myself?
faq.a: Yes — use the Community Issue Report button on this page. Your report is added to the community counter which helps other users understand whether the problem is widespread.

pages.intro: This page gives you a real-time read on New Relic's service health. As a observability and performance monitoring platform, New Relic's availability is critical — and LegalStatusCheck makes sure you're always informed.
pages.status_section:
🟢 New Relic Online — All systems operational
🟡 New Relic Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 New Relic Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: Upstream ISP or backbone routing issue
Memory leak causing gradual service degradation
Misconfigured DNS TTL causing stale resolution
Payment gateway dependency causing transaction failures
Kubernetes pod crash loop on the application cluster

faq.q: Does this page show historical uptime data?
faq.a: Yes — the uptime history grid shows the last 90 days of availability. Green cells indicate operational days, yellow indicates degraded performance, and red marks confirmed outages.

faq.q: Is it down just in my country?
faq.a: Our monitoring runs from multiple regions. If the status shows green but the service is unavailable to you, it may be a geo-restricted outage or a local ISP issue specific to your region.

faq.q: How accurate is the uptime percentage?
faq.a: The uptime percentage reflects our own monitoring data based on continuous checks. It may differ slightly from the service provider's own reported uptime metrics.

faq.q: What does 'degraded performance' mean?
faq.a: Degraded performance means the service is technically reachable but responding slowly, timing out on certain requests, or experiencing errors on some — but not all — functionality.

faq.q: Can I report an issue myself?
faq.a: Yes — use the Community Issue Report button on this page. Your report is added to the community counter which helps other users understand whether the problem is widespread.

pages.seo_title: Is Sentry Down? Sentry Status Check (Live)
pages.meta_title: Sentry Status Check – Is Sentry Down Right Now?
pages.meta_description: Check if Sentry is down or working. Live real-time Sentry status updates from LegalStatusCheck. Updated every 60 seconds.
pages.intro: Track whether Sentry is up, slow, or fully offline right now. LegalStatusCheck monitors this application error tracking and monitoring continuously and reports outages, degraded performance, and full recovery.
pages.status_section:
🟢 Sentry Online — All systems operational
🟡 Sentry Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 Sentry Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: Cross-region replication lag causing data inconsistency
Rate limiting triggered by abnormal usage patterns
OAuth or SSO provider outage blocking authentication
Webhook delivery failure causing downstream cascades
Email delivery service disruption affecting notifications

faq.q: Is this service down right now?
faq.a: Check the live status indicator at the top of this page. Green means fully operational, yellow means degraded performance, and red means a confirmed outage is in progress.

faq.q: Why is it not loading for me but working for others?
faq.a: This is usually caused by a regional outage, ISP routing issue, or a local DNS cache problem. Try flushing your DNS cache or connecting via a VPN to test from a different location.

faq.q: How often is the status data updated?
faq.a: LegalStatusCheck checks service availability every 60 seconds. The timestamp on this page shows exactly when the last check was performed.

faq.q: What causes most outages?
faq.a: The most common causes are server overload, failed deployments, DNS failures, and upstream infrastructure issues from cloud providers.

faq.q: What should I do while the service is down?
faq.a: Check the service's official status page or social media for updates. Use a cached version of your data if available, and set up an alert on this page to be notified when it recovers.

faq.q: Why does the service keep going down?
faq.a: Repeated outages usually indicate infrastructure scaling issues, ongoing incidents with a cloud provider, or architectural problems the team is actively working to resolve.

faq.q: Who do I contact if the service is down?
faq.a: Contact the service's official support team directly. While we track uptime, we have no ability to expedite recovery — only the service operator can resolve backend issues.

pages.intro: Archive.org is a Internet Archive digital library relied on by millions. When it goes offline even briefly, it causes real disruption. LegalStatusCheck tracks its uptime so you always know what's happening.
pages.status_section:
🟢 Archive.org Online — All systems operational
🟡 Archive.org Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 Archive.org Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: DDoS attack or sudden surge in request volume
Third-party API dependency failure
SSL certificate expiry or misconfiguration
Cloud infrastructure provider incident
Deployment or code push gone wrong

faq.q: Is this service down right now?
faq.a: Check the live status indicator at the top of this page. Green means fully operational, yellow means degraded performance, and red means a confirmed outage is in progress.

faq.q: Why is it not loading for me but working for others?
faq.a: This is usually caused by a regional outage, ISP routing issue, or a local DNS cache problem. Try flushing your DNS cache or connecting via a VPN to test from a different location.

faq.q: How often is the status data updated?
faq.a: LegalStatusCheck checks service availability every 60 seconds. The timestamp on this page shows exactly when the last check was performed.

faq.q: What causes most outages?
faq.a: The most common causes are server overload, failed deployments, DNS failures, and upstream infrastructure issues from cloud providers.

faq.q: What should I do while the service is down?
faq.a: Check the service's official status page or social media for updates. Use a cached version of your data if available, and set up an alert on this page to be notified when it recovers.

pages.seo_title: Is Cloudflare DNS Down? Cloudflare DNS Status Check (Live)
pages.meta_title: Cloudflare DNS Status Check – Is Cloudflare DNS Down Right N
pages.meta_description: Check if Cloudflare DNS is down or working. Live real-time Cloudflare DNS status updates from LegalStatusCheck. Updated every 60 seconds.
pages.h1: Is Cloudflare DNS down right now?
pages.intro: Not sure if Cloudflare DNS is down for everyone or just you? Our live monitoring checks Cloudflare DNS — Cloudflare public DNS resolver — every minute and reports its current operational state here.
pages.status_section:
🟢 Cloudflare DNS Online — All systems operational
🟡 Cloudflare DNS Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 Cloudflare DNS Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: CDN edge node failure or cache poisoning
Internal load balancer misconfiguration
Storage or disk I/O bottleneck on the server
Authentication service outage blocking logins
Firewall rule change blocking legitimate traffic

faq.q: How do I know if the outage is widespread?
faq.a: If our monitoring shows a red status, the issue is confirmed from multiple monitoring locations worldwide — it's not just you. Yellow status means partial or regional issues.

faq.q: Can I get notified when it comes back online?
faq.a: Yes — LegalStatusCheck offers status alerts. Bookmark this page and check back, or use the notification feature to receive an alert when service is restored.

faq.q: Is the status check real-time?
faq.a: Our checks run every minute from multiple global locations. The status you see reflects the most recent check, never more than 60 seconds old.

faq.q: Why does the service keep going down?
faq.a: Repeated outages usually indicate infrastructure scaling issues, ongoing incidents with a cloud provider, or architectural problems the team is actively working to resolve.

faq.q: Who do I contact if the service is down?
faq.a: Contact the service's official support team directly. While we track uptime, we have no ability to expedite recovery — only the service operator can resolve backend issues.

faq.q: Does this page show historical uptime data?
faq.a: Yes — the uptime history grid shows the last 90 days of availability. Green cells indicate operational days, yellow indicates degraded performance, and red marks confirmed outages.

faq.q: Is it down just in my country?
faq.a: Our monitoring runs from multiple regions. If the status shows green but the service is unavailable to you, it may be a geo-restricted outage or a local ISP issue specific to your region.

faq.q: How accurate is the uptime percentage?
faq.a: The uptime percentage reflects our own monitoring data based on continuous checks. It may differ slightly from the service provider's own reported uptime metrics.

faq.q: What does 'degraded performance' mean?
faq.a: Degraded performance means the service is technically reachable but responding slowly, timing out on certain requests, or experiencing errors on some — but not all — functionality.

faq.q: Can I report an issue myself?
faq.a: Yes — use the Community Issue Report button on this page. Your report is added to the community counter which helps other users understand whether the problem is widespread.

pages.meta_description: Check if Regex101 is down or working. Live real-time Regex101 status updates from LegalStatusCheck. Updated every 60 seconds.
pages.intro: If Regex101 isn't loading for you, you're in the right place. LegalStatusCheck provides live uptime data for Regex101, a online regular expression tester, updated continuously.
pages.status_section:
🟢 Regex101 Online — All systems operational
🟡 Regex101 Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 Regex101 Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: CDN edge node failure or cache poisoning
Internal load balancer misconfiguration
Storage or disk I/O bottleneck on the server
Authentication service outage blocking logins
Firewall rule change blocking legitimate traffic

faq.q: How do I know if the outage is widespread?
faq.a: If our monitoring shows a red status, the issue is confirmed from multiple monitoring locations worldwide — it's not just you. Yellow status means partial or regional issues.

faq.q: Can I get notified when it comes back online?
faq.a: Yes — LegalStatusCheck offers status alerts. Bookmark this page and check back, or use the notification feature to receive an alert when service is restored.

faq.q: Is the status check real-time?
faq.a: Our checks run every minute from multiple global locations. The status you see reflects the most recent check, never more than 60 seconds old.

faq.q: Why does the service keep going down?
faq.a: Repeated outages usually indicate infrastructure scaling issues, ongoing incidents with a cloud provider, or architectural problems the team is actively working to resolve.

faq.q: Who do I contact if the service is down?
faq.a: Contact the service's official support team directly. While we track uptime, we have no ability to expedite recovery — only the service operator can resolve backend issues.

pages.seo_title: Is JSONFormatter Down? JSONFormatter Status Check (Live)
pages.meta_title: JSONFormatter Status Check – Is JSONFormatter Down Right Now
pages.meta_description: Check if JSONFormatter is down or working. Live real-time JSONFormatter status updates from LegalStatusCheck. Updated every 60 seconds.
pages.intro: Is JSONFormatter down or is it just you? This page gives you a real-time answer. We track JSONFormatter — online JSON formatting and validation tool — and surface any outages or slowdowns the moment they occur.
pages.status_section:
🟢 JSONFormatter Online — All systems operational
🟡 JSONFormatter Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 JSONFormatter Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: Upstream ISP or backbone routing issue
Memory leak causing gradual service degradation
Misconfigured DNS TTL causing stale resolution
Payment gateway dependency causing transaction failures
Kubernetes pod crash loop on the application cluster

faq.q: Does this page show historical uptime data?
faq.a: Yes — the uptime history grid shows the last 90 days of availability. Green cells indicate operational days, yellow indicates degraded performance, and red marks confirmed outages.

faq.q: Is it down just in my country?
faq.a: Our monitoring runs from multiple regions. If the status shows green but the service is unavailable to you, it may be a geo-restricted outage or a local ISP issue specific to your region.

faq.q: How accurate is the uptime percentage?
faq.a: The uptime percentage reflects our own monitoring data based on continuous checks. It may differ slightly from the service provider's own reported uptime metrics.

faq.q: What does 'degraded performance' mean?
faq.a: Degraded performance means the service is technically reachable but responding slowly, timing out on certain requests, or experiencing errors on some — but not all — functionality.

faq.q: Can I report an issue myself?
faq.a: Yes — use the Community Issue Report button on this page. Your report is added to the community counter which helps other users understand whether the problem is widespread.

faq.a: Check the live status indicator at the top of this page. Green means fully operational, yellow means degraded performance, and red means a confirmed outage is in progress.

faq.q: Why is it not loading for me but working for others?
faq.a: This is usually caused by a regional outage, ISP routing issue, or a local DNS cache problem. Try flushing your DNS cache or connecting via a VPN to test from a different location.

faq.q: How often is the status data updated?
faq.a: LegalStatusCheck checks service availability every 60 seconds. The timestamp on this page shows exactly when the last check was performed.

faq.q: What causes most outages?
faq.a: The most common causes are server overload, failed deployments, DNS failures, and upstream infrastructure issues from cloud providers.

faq.q: What should I do while the service is down?
faq.a: Check the service's official status page or social media for updates. Use a cached version of your data if available, and set up an alert on this page to be notified when it recovers.

pages.h1: What to do when your SaaS tool is down
pages.intro: It happens to every team eventually — a critical SaaS tool stops working right in the middle of your busiest period. Here's exactly what to do when it happens to minimise the impact and get back on track fast.
pages.status_section: N/A — support article
why_down: Unexpected infrastructure failure at the cloud provider
Botched deployment introducing a critical bug
Database overload caused by a traffic spike
Third-party API dependency going offline
Security incident triggering an emergency takedown

faq.q: How long do SaaS outages usually last?
faq.a: Most SaaS outages are resolved within 15 minutes to 2 hours. Major infrastructure incidents can last longer, but most providers have SLAs committing to restoration within 4 hours.

faq.q: Should I contact SaaS support during an outage?
faq.a: Check the service's status page first. If a known incident is already posted, support teams are already working on it and contacting them won't speed up recovery.

faq.q: How do I prepare my team for SaaS downtime?
faq.a: Document offline fallback procedures, maintain local copies of critical data, and identify alternative tools for core functions in advance of any outage.

pages.seo_title: Why Websites Go Down — The Most Common Causes Explained
pages.meta_title: Why Do Websites Go Down? Common Causes | LegalStatusCheck
pages.meta_description: Learn the most common reasons websites go offline — from server overload to DNS failures — and what site owners do to recover.
pages.intro: Every website goes down eventually. Understanding why helps you diagnose outages faster and manage your expectations as a user. Here are the most common causes of website downtime, from the mundane to the catastrophic.
pages.status_section: N/A — support article
why_down: Server hardware failure or data centre fire
Traffic spike exceeding server capacity
Accidental deletion of critical configuration files
Unpatched security vulnerability being actively exploited
ISP or DNS provider outage outside the site owner's control

faq.q: What is the most common cause of website downtime?
faq.a: Server overload and failed deployments are statistically the most common causes of unplanned downtime. DNS failures are the most common cause of sites appearing down when the servers are actually fine.

faq.q: How can I tell if a website is down for everyone?
faq.a: Use LegalStatusCheck to see if our global monitoring detects an outage. If we show the site as online but you can't reach it, the issue is likely local to your network.

faq.q: Does downtime affect SEO?
faq.a: Brief outages of under an hour rarely impact SEO. Extended outages of 24+ hours can cause search engines to temporarily deindex pages, though they typically recover once the site is restored.

pages.seo_title: How To Check If a Website Is Down (5 Fast Methods)
pages.meta_title: How To Check If a Website Is Down | LegalStatusCheck
pages.meta_description: 5 fast ways to check if a website is down — from using LegalStatusCheck to running a ping test and checking DNS resolution.
pages.h1: How to check if a website is down
pages.intro: Can't load a website and not sure if it's down for everyone or just you? There are several quick ways to find out. This guide walks you through the five fastest and most reliable methods — no technical knowledge required.
pages.status_section: N/A — support article
why_down: DNS cache on your device serving an outdated IP address
ISP routing issue preventing packets reaching the server
Browser extension blocking the site incorrectly
Corporate firewall or VPN redirecting your traffic
Server returning an error only to certain user agents

faq.q: What is the fastest way to check if a site is down?
faq.a: The fastest method is to search the site name on LegalStatusCheck — our global monitoring checks the site every 60 seconds from multiple locations and gives you an instant answer.

faq.q: Why can my colleague access a site but I can't?
faq.a: This usually means the site is up globally but something between you and the server is failing — your ISP's routing, a local DNS cache, a VPN, or a browser extension.

faq.q: Does clearing cache fix website loading issues?
faq.a: Sometimes. If the site was previously down and your browser cached the error page, clearing cache and cookies forces a fresh request and can resolve the issue once the site recovers.

pages.seo_title: Is It Down for Everyone or Just Me? How to Find Out Fast
pages.meta_title: Down for Everyone or Just Me? | LegalStatusCheck
pages.meta_description: Find out if a website is down for everyone or just you. Quick diagnosis guide covering DNS, ISP issues, VPN problems and browser cache.
pages.h1: Is it down for everyone or just me?
pages.intro: One of the most frustrating experiences online: a website won't load and you have no idea if it's a global outage or something specific to your setup. This guide helps you figure it out in under two minutes.
pages.status_section: N/A — support article
why_down: Your ISP has a routing issue to that specific server
Your DNS resolver is returning a stale or incorrect IP
A VPN is routing your traffic through a blocked region
Your browser has cached a previous error response
A corporate proxy is incorrectly blocking the domain

faq.q: How do I tell if a site is down just for me?
faq.a: Check LegalStatusCheck first — if we show the site as online, the problem is on your end. Try a different browser, disable your VPN, flush your DNS cache, or test from your phone's mobile data.

faq.q: What does 'DNS_PROBE_FINISHED_NXDOMAIN' mean?
faq.a: This Chrome error means your DNS resolver can't find the domain. The site may be down, or your DNS cache may be stale. Try flushing your DNS or switching to a public DNS like 8.8.8.8.

faq.q: If I can access a site on mobile data but not Wi-Fi, what does that mean?
faq.a: It means the problem is with your home or office network — not the website. Your router's DNS cache or your ISP's routing is likely at fault.

internal_links.text: How to Check if a Website is Down

pages.seo_title: What Is Website Uptime and Why Does It Matter?
pages.meta_title: What Is Uptime? Why It Matters for Websites | LegalStatusCheck
pages.meta_description: Understand what website uptime means, how it's measured as a percentage, and why even 99% uptime means over 3 days of downtime per year.
pages.h1: What is website uptime and why does it matter?
pages.intro: Uptime is the percentage of time a website or service is operational and accessible to users. It sounds simple, but the math behind uptime percentages reveals just how much downtime even highly available services can experience in practice.
pages.status_section: N/A — support article
why_down: Insufficient server redundancy causing single points of failure
No automatic failover configured for database outages
Missing health checks allowing degraded pods to serve traffic
Lack of load balancing creating bottlenecks under traffic spikes
No CDN in place leaving origin servers exposed to direct overload

faq.q: What does 99.9% uptime actually mean?
faq.a: 99.9% uptime allows for approximately 8.7 hours of downtime per year, 43.8 minutes per month, or about 10 minutes per week. It sounds high but still means meaningful disruption.

faq.q: What is a good uptime percentage for a SaaS tool?
faq.a: Enterprise SaaS tools typically target 99.9% to 99.99% uptime. 99.99% (four nines) allows only about 52 minutes of downtime per year and is considered best-in-class.

faq.q: How does LegalStatusCheck measure uptime?
faq.a: LegalStatusCheck measures uptime by sending HTTP requests to monitored services every 60 seconds from multiple global monitoring locations. A service is marked down when it fails to respond within our timeout threshold.

faq.q: What does 'degraded performance' mean?
faq.a: Degraded performance means the service is technically reachable but responding slowly, timing out on certain requests, or experiencing errors on some — but not all — functionality.

faq.q: Can I report an issue myself?
faq.a: Yes — use the Community Issue Report button on this page. Your report is added to the community counter which helps other users understand whether the problem is widespread.

🔴 iCIMS Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: DDoS attack or sudden surge in request volume
Third-party API dependency failure
SSL certificate expiry or misconfiguration
Cloud infrastructure provider incident
Deployment or code push gone wrong

faq.q: Is this service down right now?
faq.a: Check the live status indicator at the top of this page. Green means fully operational, yellow means degraded performance, and red means a confirmed outage is in progress.

faq.q: Why is it not loading for me but working for others?
faq.a: This is usually caused by a regional outage, ISP routing issue, or a local DNS cache problem. Try flushing your DNS cache or connecting via a VPN to test from a different location.

faq.q: How often is the status data updated?
faq.a: LegalStatusCheck checks service availability every 60 seconds. The timestamp on this page shows exactly when the last check was performed.

faq.q: What causes most outages?
faq.a: The most common causes are server overload, failed deployments, DNS failures, and upstream infrastructure issues from cloud providers.

faq.q: What should I do while the service is down?
faq.a: Check the service's official status page or social media for updates. Use a cached version of your data if available, and set up an alert on this page to be notified when it recovers.

faq.q: How do I know if the outage is widespread?
faq.a: If our monitoring shows a red status, the issue is confirmed from multiple monitoring locations worldwide — it's not just you. Yellow status means partial or regional issues.

faq.q: Is the status check real-time?
faq.a: Our checks run every minute from multiple global locations. The status you see reflects the most recent check, never more than 60 seconds old.

faq.q: Why does the service keep going down?
faq.a: Repeated outages usually indicate infrastructure scaling issues, ongoing incidents with a cloud provider, or architectural problems the team is actively working to resolve.

faq.q: Who do I contact if the service is down?
faq.a: Contact the service's official support team directly. While we track uptime, we have no ability to expedite recovery — only the service operator can resolve backend issues.

faq.q: Does this page show historical uptime data?
faq.a: Yes — the uptime history grid shows the last 90 days of availability. Green cells indicate operational days, yellow indicates degraded performance, and red marks confirmed outages.

faq.q: Is it down just in my country?
faq.a: Our monitoring runs from multiple regions. If the status shows green but the service is unavailable to you, it may be a geo-restricted outage or a local ISP issue specific to your region.

faq.q: How accurate is the uptime percentage?
faq.a: The uptime percentage reflects our own monitoring data based on continuous checks. It may differ slightly from the service provider's own reported uptime metrics.

faq.q: What does 'degraded performance' mean?
faq.a: Degraded performance means the service is technically reachable but responding slowly, timing out on certain requests, or experiencing errors on some — but not all — functionality.

faq.q: Can I report an issue myself?
faq.a: Yes — use the Community Issue Report button on this page. Your report is added to the community counter which helps other users understand whether the problem is widespread.

pages.intro: Check Honeycomb's current service status here. Honeycomb is a observability platform for distributed systems and LegalStatusCheck keeps tabs on its uptime, response time, and any reported disruptions.
pages.status_section:
🟢 Honeycomb Online — All systems operational
🟡 Honeycomb Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 Honeycomb Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: Cross-region replication lag causing data inconsistency
Rate limiting triggered by abnormal usage patterns
OAuth or SSO provider outage blocking authentication
Webhook delivery failure causing downstream cascades
Email delivery service disruption affecting notifications

faq.q: Does this page show historical uptime data?
faq.a: Yes — the uptime history grid shows the last 90 days of availability. Green cells indicate operational days, yellow indicates degraded performance, and red marks confirmed outages.

faq.q: Is it down just in my country?
faq.a: Our monitoring runs from multiple regions. If the status shows green but the service is unavailable to you, it may be a geo-restricted outage or a local ISP issue specific to your region.

faq.q: How accurate is the uptime percentage?
faq.a: The uptime percentage reflects our own monitoring data based on continuous checks. It may differ slightly from the service provider's own reported uptime metrics.

faq.q: What does 'degraded performance' mean?
faq.a: Degraded performance means the service is technically reachable but responding slowly, timing out on certain requests, or experiencing errors on some — but not all — functionality.

faq.q: Can I report an issue myself?
faq.a: Yes — use the Community Issue Report button on this page. Your report is added to the community counter which helps other users understand whether the problem is widespread.

pages.seo_title: Is Jaeger Down? Jaeger Status Check (Live)
pages.meta_title: Jaeger Status Check – Is Jaeger Down Right Now?
pages.meta_description: Check if Jaeger is down or working. Live real-time Jaeger status updates from LegalStatusCheck. Updated every 60 seconds.
pages.intro: Having trouble accessing Jaeger? This status page pulls live monitoring data for Jaeger, open-source distributed tracing system, and tells you whether the issue is widespread or isolated to your connection.
pages.status_section:
🟢 Jaeger Online — All systems operational
🟡 Jaeger Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 Jaeger Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: Server overload caused by an unexpected traffic spike
Scheduled or emergency maintenance window
DNS resolution failure preventing domain lookup
Regional network outage affecting specific geographies
Database connectivity issue on the backend

faq.q: Is this service down right now?
faq.a: Check the live status indicator at the top of this page. Green means fully operational, yellow means degraded performance, and red means a confirmed outage is in progress.

faq.q: Why is it not loading for me but working for others?
faq.a: This is usually caused by a regional outage, ISP routing issue, or a local DNS cache problem. Try flushing your DNS cache or connecting via a VPN to test from a different location.

faq.q: How often is the status data updated?
faq.a: LegalStatusCheck checks service availability every 60 seconds. The timestamp on this page shows exactly when the last check was performed.

faq.q: What causes most outages?
faq.a: The most common causes are server overload, failed deployments, DNS failures, and upstream infrastructure issues from cloud providers.

faq.q: What should I do while the service is down?
faq.a: Check the service's official status page or social media for updates. Use a cached version of your data if available, and set up an alert on this page to be notified when it recovers.

faq.a: Repeated outages usually indicate infrastructure scaling issues, ongoing incidents with a cloud provider, or architectural problems the team is actively working to resolve.

faq.q: How do I know if the outage is widespread?
faq.a: If our monitoring shows a red status, the issue is confirmed from multiple monitoring locations worldwide — it's not just you. Yellow status means partial or regional issues.

faq.q: Is the status check real-time?
faq.a: Our checks run every minute from multiple global locations. The status you see reflects the most recent check, never more than 60 seconds old.

faq.q: Why does the service keep going down?
faq.a: Repeated outages usually indicate infrastructure scaling issues, ongoing incidents with a cloud provider, or architectural problems the team is actively working to resolve.

faq.q: Who do I contact if the service is down?
faq.a: Contact the service's official support team directly. While we track uptime, we have no ability to expedite recovery — only the service operator can resolve backend issues.

pages.status_section:

🔴 NBA 2K Online Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: CDN edge node failure or cache poisoning
Internal load balancer misconfiguration
Storage or disk I/O bottleneck on the server
Authentication service outage blocking logins
Firewall rule change blocking legitimate traffic

faq.q: Does this page show historical uptime data?
faq.a: Yes — the uptime history grid shows the last 90 days of availability. Green cells indicate operational days, yellow indicates degraded performance, and red marks confirmed outages.

faq.q: Is it down just in my country?
faq.a: Our monitoring runs from multiple regions. If the status shows green but the service is unavailable to you, it may be a geo-restricted outage or a local ISP issue specific to your region.

faq.q: How accurate is the uptime percentage?
faq.a: The uptime percentage reflects our own monitoring data based on continuous checks. It may differ slightly from the service provider's own reported uptime metrics.

faq.q: What does 'degraded performance' mean?
faq.a: Degraded performance means the service is technically reachable but responding slowly, timing out on certain requests, or experiencing errors on some — but not all — functionality.

faq.q: Can I report an issue myself?
faq.a: Yes — use the Community Issue Report button on this page. Your report is added to the community counter which helps other users understand whether the problem is widespread.

pages.h1: Is Craft Docs down right now?
pages.intro: Check Craft Docs's current service status here. Craft Docs is a document and notes editor for Apple and LegalStatusCheck keeps tabs on its uptime, response time, and any reported disruptions.
pages.status_section:
🟢 Craft Docs Online — All systems operational
🟡 Craft Docs Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 Craft Docs Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: Cross-region replication lag causing data inconsistency
Rate limiting triggered by abnormal usage patterns
OAuth or SSO provider outage blocking authentication
Webhook delivery failure causing downstream cascades
Email delivery service disruption affecting notifications

faq.q: How do I know if the outage is widespread?
faq.a: If our monitoring shows a red status, the issue is confirmed from multiple monitoring locations worldwide — it's not just you. Yellow status means partial or regional issues.

faq.q: Can I get notified when it comes back online?
faq.a: Yes — LegalStatusCheck offers status alerts. Bookmark this page and check back, or use the notification feature to receive an alert when service is restored.

faq.q: Is the status check real-time?
faq.a: Our checks run every minute from multiple global locations. The status you see reflects the most recent check, never more than 60 seconds old.

faq.q: Why does the service keep going down?
faq.a: Repeated outages usually indicate infrastructure scaling issues, ongoing incidents with a cloud provider, or architectural problems the team is actively working to resolve.

faq.q: Who do I contact if the service is down?
faq.a: Contact the service's official support team directly. While we track uptime, we have no ability to expedite recovery — only the service operator can resolve backend issues.

pages.seo_title: Is Coda Down? Coda Status Check (Live)
pages.meta_title: Coda Status Check – Is Coda Down Right Now?
pages.meta_description: Check if Coda is down or working. Live real-time Coda status updates from LegalStatusCheck. Updated every 60 seconds.
pages.intro: Having trouble accessing Coda? This status page pulls live monitoring data for Coda, all-in-one document and data platform, and tells you whether the issue is widespread or isolated to your connection.
pages.status_section:
🟢 Coda Online — All systems operational
🟡 Coda Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 Coda Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: Server overload caused by an unexpected traffic spike
Scheduled or emergency maintenance window
DNS resolution failure preventing domain lookup
Regional network outage affecting specific geographies
Database connectivity issue on the backend

faq.q: Does this page show historical uptime data?
faq.a: Yes — the uptime history grid shows the last 90 days of availability. Green cells indicate operational days, yellow indicates degraded performance, and red marks confirmed outages.

faq.q: Is it down just in my country?
faq.a: Our monitoring runs from multiple regions. If the status shows green but the service is unavailable to you, it may be a geo-restricted outage or a local ISP issue specific to your region.

faq.q: How accurate is the uptime percentage?
faq.a: The uptime percentage reflects our own monitoring data based on continuous checks. It may differ slightly from the service provider's own reported uptime metrics.

faq.q: What does 'degraded performance' mean?
faq.a: Degraded performance means the service is technically reachable but responding slowly, timing out on certain requests, or experiencing errors on some — but not all — functionality.

faq.q: Can I report an issue myself?
faq.a: Yes — use the Community Issue Report button on this page. Your report is added to the community counter which helps other users understand whether the problem is widespread.

why_down: DDoS attack or sudden surge in request volume
Third-party API dependency failure
SSL certificate expiry or misconfiguration
Cloud infrastructure provider incident
Deployment or code push gone wrong

faq.q: Is this service down right now?
faq.a: Check the live status indicator at the top of this page. Green means fully operational, yellow means degraded performance, and red means a confirmed outage is in progress.

faq.q: Why is it not loading for me but working for others?
faq.a: This is usually caused by a regional outage, ISP routing issue, or a local DNS cache problem. Try flushing your DNS cache or connecting via a VPN to test from a different location.

faq.q: How often is the status data updated?
faq.a: LegalStatusCheck checks service availability every 60 seconds. The timestamp on this page shows exactly when the last check was performed.

faq.q: What causes most outages?
faq.a: The most common causes are server overload, failed deployments, DNS failures, and upstream infrastructure issues from cloud providers.

faq.q: What should I do while the service is down?
faq.a: Check the service's official status page or social media for updates. Use a cached version of your data if available, and set up an alert on this page to be notified when it recovers.

pages.intro: Having trouble accessing Wyze? This status page pulls live monitoring data for Wyze, affordable smart home device platform, and tells you whether the issue is widespread or isolated to your connection.
pages.status_section:
🟢 Wyze Online — All systems operational
🟡 Wyze Slow — Degraded performance or partial outage
🔴 Wyze Down — Major outage in progress
why_down: Server overload caused by an unexpected traffic spike
Scheduled or emergency maintenance window
DNS resolution failure preventing domain lookup
Regional network outage affecting specific geographies
Database connectivity issue on the backend

faq.q: Does this page show historical uptime data?
faq.a: Yes — the uptime history grid shows the last 90 days of availability. Green cells indicate operational days, yellow indicates degraded performance, and red marks confirmed outages.

faq.q: Is it down just in my country?
faq.a: Our monitoring runs from multiple regions. If the status shows green but the service is unavailable to you, it may be a geo-restricted outage or a local ISP issue specific to your region.

faq.q: How accurate is the uptime percentage?
faq.a: The uptime percentage reflects our own monitoring data based on continuous checks. It may differ slightly from the service provider's own reported uptime metrics.

faq.q: What does 'degraded performance' mean?
faq.a: Degraded performance means the service is technically reachable but responding slowly, timing out on certain requests, or experiencing errors on some — but not all — functionality.

faq.q: Can I report an issue myself?
faq.a: Yes — use the Community Issue Report button on this page. Your report is added to the community counter which helps other users understand whether the problem is widespread.

Third-party API dependency failure
SSL certificate expiry or misconfiguration

faq.q: Is this service down right now?
faq.a: Check the live status indicator at the top of this page. Green means fully operational, yellow means degraded performance, and red means a confirmed outage is in progress.

faq.q: Why is it not loading for me but working for others?
faq.a: This is usually caused by a regional outage, ISP routing issue, or a local DNS cache problem. Try flushing your DNS cache or connecting via a VPN to test from a different location.

faq.q: How often is the status data updated?
faq.a: LegalStatusCheck checks service availability every 60 seconds. The timestamp on this page shows exactly when the last check was performed.

faq.q: What causes most outages?
faq.a: The most common causes are server overload, failed deployments, DNS failures, and upstream infrastructure issues from cloud providers.

faq.q: What should I do while the service is down?
faq.a: Check the service's official status page or social media for updates. Use a cached version of your data if available, and set up an alert on this page to be notified when it recovers.

faq.q: Why does the service keep going down?
faq.a: Repeated outages usually indicate infrastructure scaling issues, ongoing incidents with a cloud provider, or architectural problems the team is actively working to resolve.

faq.q: Who do I contact if the service is down?
